item.title: Data centers and Nevada's dwindling resources
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Communities nationwide are speaking out against data centers, yet more projects keep getting proposed.
It's especially concerning in states running low on water and power, like Nevada. News creator Stephanie Valdez explains why this year's midterm elections could determine the future of data centers and resources. #datacenters #evaporativecooling #midtermelections
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: Everything You Didn’t Know About The Titanic | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
...Think you know EVERYTHING there is to know about the Titanic? Think Again!! Today on Weird History, we go deep.. deep into facts and details about the Titanic. What were the standard hygienic practices on the ship? What did the official Titanic menu have on it? Also, how exactly did it get discovered, and what's with that whole 'Titanic II' project? These, and many other wild aspect all surrounding The Titanic. What are your favorite things about the Titanic? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
Chapters: 00:00:00 - What Hygiene Was Like On the Titanic 00:10:47 - How the Titanic Was Discovered During a Secret Cold War Mission 00:21:34 - What Did Passengers Eat On The Titanic? 00:34:15 - CRAZY Facts About Titanic II 00:44:31 - What Circumstances Led to the Titanic Sinking? 00:55:55 - Based On Your Income, Would You Have Survived the Titanic Disaster?
#titanic #compilation #weirdhistory

item.title: Why are young men turning to steroids? | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Bigorexia, or muscle dysmorphia, is a growing concern among young men. No matter how much muscle they build, many never see themselves as big enough.
As SARMs and anabolic steroids become easier to find online — often marketed like ordinary supplements and promoted across social media — they've become increasingly visible to young men. More are taking serious risks in pursuit of the "ideal" physique, and some have paid for it with their health.
One statistic is particularly striking: steroid dependence rates are estimated to be higher than heroin, cocaine, or alcohol.
Watch the full documentary "Muscle mania and the cult of masculinity - When the body becomes an obsession" on the DW Documentary YouTube channel.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. Subscribe and explore the world around you with DW Documentary.
Subscribe to: ‬ ⮞ DW Documentary (English): https://www.youtube.com/@DWDocumentary ⮞ DW Documental (Spanish): https://www.youtube.com/@DWDocumental ⮞ DW Documentary وثائقية دي دبليو (Arabic): https://www.youtube.com/@DWDocarabia ⮞ DW Documentary हिन्दी (Hindi): https://www.youtube.com/@DWDochindi ⮞ DW Dokumenter (Indonesian): https://www.youtube.com/@DWDokumenter ⮞ DW Doku (German): https://www.youtube.com/@DWDoku
For more visit: http://www.dw.com/en/tv/docfilm/s-3610 Follow DW Documentary on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwdocumentary/ Follow DW Documental on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/dwdocumental

item.title: The MAGA stars freaked out by their own movement | The Gray Area
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
For years, right-wing influencers and politicians have pushed the conservative movement to have more controversial, anti-establishment views. Why are some of those figures now upset about where the movement has ended up?
Subscribe to our channel! http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
In today’s episode, guest host Bryan Walsh speaks with Vox Senior Correspondent Zack Beauchamp about conservatives who decry the extremist direction that the right is moving but refuse to recognize the role they played in pushing the movement in that direction. The two discuss US Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY), pundit Ben Shapiro, podcaster Candace Owens, the drivers of conservative extremism, and what the party might look like after Donald Trump.
Guest Host: Bryan Walsh, Vox Senior Editorial Director Guest: Zack Beauchamp, Vox Senior Correspondent
Read Zack’s essay: The MAGA stars freaked out by their own movement
00:00 Intro 01:54 Who are the ‘hot dog men?’ 03:41 US Rep. Thomas Massie and President Trump 07:26 Ben Shapiro and Candace Owens 16:23 Why scandal doesn’t stick to President Trump 23:04 Why the America Right keeps moving towards more controversial views 30:17 Anti-semitism and American politics 36:48 The future of the conservative movement
We would love to hear from you. To tell us what you thought of this episode, email us at thegrayarea@vox.com or leave us a voicemail at 1-800-214-5749. Your comments and questions help us make a better show.
And you can watch new episodes of The Gray Area on YouTube.
Listen to The Gray Area ad-free by becoming a Vox Member: vox.com/members
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on Facebook: http://goo.gl/U2g06o Or Twitter: http://goo.gl/XFrZ5H

item.title: Inside Pakistan's High-Stakes Gamble on Trump
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
Pakistan is enjoying a moment in the diplomatic sun as an intermediary in the US-Israel war with Iran. By cultivating ties with Donald Trump, its leaders may open a path to more economic opportunity.
Explore these and other related stories in more depth on Bloomberg.com:
Pakistan Signs Deal With US to Revamp NYC’s Roosevelt HOtel https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-02-19/pakistan-signs-deal-with-us-to-revamp-nyc-s-roosevelt-hotel
Pakistan Backs Trump for Nobel Peace Prize He’s Long Craved https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-06-21/pakistan-backs-trump-for-nobel-peace-prize-he-s-long-craved
Rubio Seeks to Repair US-India Relations After Trump-Era Strains https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-05-26/rubio-tries-to-mend-india-ties-after-trump-roiled-relations
00:00 Intro 01:43 Titlecard 01:53 Pakistan Key Figures 02:33 Trump Diplomatic Approach 02:57 Pakistan’s Economy 03:45 Balancing Act with China and the US 04:16 US-Pakistan Tensions 05:40 Business Partnerships 06:54 Pakistan as Mediator 07:47 High-Level Talks 08:26 Future Economic Stakes 09:08 Conclusion
#Pakistan #Trump #US

item.title: Make America Charlie Kirk
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: Hitler's Death Army: The Complete Story Of The Das Reich SS Division
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
On June 6, 1944, the elite Second SS Panzer Division Das Reich was ordered to the Normandy front. As they marched across France, they left a horrific trail of massacres, including the complete destruction of Oradour-sur-Glane. Delayed by courageous French Resistance and SOE operatives like Violette Szabo, the division arrived too late to stop the Allied invasion and was ultimately decimated. Discover the dark history of Hitler's most brutal division and the controversial post-war trials that followed their escape from justice.
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more. Get an exclusive release every week by signing up here: https://bit.ly/4pyExyn
This channel is part of the History Hit Network. Any queries, please contact owned-enquiries@littledotstudios.com

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Trump's USAID Cuts | Exclusive Preview
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
When Donald Trump abruptly dismantled the US Agency for International Development, aid groups and researchers warned millions of lives would be lost. Bloomberg Investigates travels to Kenya to examine the consequences of the cuts by Trump and his deputy, Elon Musk, and how unfinished projects may be creating new dangers.
Dozens of American initiatives designed to provide clean water and protect communities from flooding have been abandoned, leaving some of the country's most vulnerable people at even greater risk than before.
Watch the full documentary: https://bloom.bg/4ylmAaF
Bloomberg Investigates is an award-winning series that sets out to uncover the truth behind some of the most powerful stories from around the world.
#Trump #USAID #Kenya
-------- Like this video? Subscribe: http://www.youtube.com/Bloomberg?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: The Complete Story of Ancient Greece | Odyssey, Troy, Myths & Warfare
item.author: History Hit
item.content:encoded:
Obsessed with the Odyssey? Learn more about ancient Greek myths, people and warfare from our historians Roel Konijnendijk, Natalie Haynes, Honor Cargill-Martin and Tristan Hughes.
00:00 Introduction 00:32 Medusa with Natalie Haynes Episode 1 34:29 Medusa with Natalie Haynes Episode 2 59:45 Greek Warfare with Roel Konijnendijk 01:33:19 Greek Women with Honour Cargill-Martin 02:00:32 Agree To Disagree with Tristan and Roel

item.title: America’s Scientists Are Disappearing
item.author: StarTalk
item.content:encoded:
Are we risking an America where we no longer innovate? Neil deGrasse Tyson breaks down the massive cuts to science funding, how it’s fuelling an American brain drain, and what that means for the future of innovation in America.
Timestamps: 00:00 - Back in Grad School 01:57 - The Government’s Science Cuts 05:06 - The Unpredictable Innovation from Basic Research 09:18 - The Brain Drain 14:40 - How We Fix It
Check out our second channel, @StarTalkPlus

#StarTalk #NeildeGrasseTyson

item.title: The Surprising Power of Sexual Selection | Evolution | BBC Earth Science
item.author: BBC Earth Science
item.content:encoded:
Chris Packham explains why, from an evolutionary perspective, the ability to win over a mate can be just as important as staying alive.
Find out where to watch Evolution where you are 👉 https://www.bbcearth.com/shows/evolution

item.title: Is the war in Iran really over?
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
President Trump declared the war with Iran over. Months later, the fighting continues.
Tom Nichols, a staff writer at The Atlantic and retired professor at the U.S. Naval War College, explains why he believes the administration's public claims about the war don't match what's happening on the ground — and why Americans deserve a clearer explanation of where this war is headed.
Watch the full episode of Today, Explained: https://open.spotify.com/episode/7gCISlzrliBJVrsfgsvAIq?si=34e4f43c71614e51
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.title: Great advice from an astronaut 🧑🏿‍🚀⭐🚀
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
Former astronaut Charles Bolden recalled an encounter in the late ’70s with Ron McNair, one of the first African American astronauts in NASA history, that challenged what he believed was possible. When Charles said he didn’t think he’d be chosen for the space shuttle program, Ron pushed back with a simple question that would change his life: “How do you know if you don’t ask?” Charles would go on to become part of the second group of space shuttle astronauts — and later the first African American to lead NASA.
Stream “Once Upon a Time in Space" on the PBS app!
Made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app!
#astronaut #space #spaceexploration #pilot #advice

item.title: Weirdest Concealed Firearm Ever Made? The Equalizer Pistol Ring with expert Jonathan Ferguson
item.author: Royal Armouries
item.content:encoded:
It looks like an oversized ring... but it's actually a firearm.
In this episode, Jonathan examines a concealed gun in jewellery form.
We explore how it works, why it was invented, the patent behind it and whether it ever made practical sense.
00:00 A Ring... or a Gun? 01:20 How the Ring Gun Fires 03:26 Can You Actually Aim It? 04:44 Safety Features & External Design 07:07 Inside the Mechanism 10:52 The Patent 12:12 Why Was It Invented? 15:44 The Improved Design 16:52 Brilliant Idea or Bad Weapon?
Object Number: PR.9817 https://royalarmouries.org/collection/object/object-280068
Sources/Reading:
Patent EP0156061A2 Personal protection firearm’ https://worldwide.espacenet.com/patent/search?q=pn%3DEP0156061A2
Improved patent https://worldwide.espacenet.com/patent/search?q=pn%3DUS4748759A
Subscribe to our channel for more videos about arms and armour
Help us bring history to life by supporting us here: https://royalarmouries.org/support-us/donations/
Sign up to our museum membership scheme here: https://royalarmouries.org/support-us/membership/
⚔Website: https://royalarmouries.org/home ⚔Blog: https://royalarmouries.org/stories/ ⚔Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/RoyalArmouriesMuseum/ ⚔Twitter: https://twitter.com/Royal_Armouries ⚔ Instagram: http://instagram.com/royalarmouriesmuseum
We are the Royal Armouries, the United Kingdom's national collection of arms and armour. Discover what goes on behind the scenes and watch our collection come to life. See combat demonstrations, experience jousting and meet our experts.
Have a question about arms and armour? Feel free to leave us a comment and we'll do our best to answer it.

item.title: Why is Trump obsessed with election fraud?
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump just can’t let the 2020 election go — and it may impact his performance in the upcoming midterms. Today, Explained host Sean Rameswaram talks about why Trump is spending time on election fraud instead of issues plaguing Americans.
Listen to the full episode of Today, Explained wherever you get your podcasts.

item.author: Kurzgesagt – In a Nutshell
item.content:encoded:
Sugar, salt, cinnamon, saffron... which common kitchen ingredient is the deadliest?

item.title: AI, Corporate Responsibility & Democratic Legitimacy: Extended Q&A • Joanna Bryson • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This Q&A session was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Joanna Bryson - Professor of Ethics and Technology, Responsible Data Science for Human Development @j2bryson
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/j2bryson.bsky.social https://mastodon.social/@j2bryson https://twitter.com/j2bryson https://www.linkedin.com/in/bryson https://joanna-bryson.blogspot.com
Links https://digital-markets-act.ec.europa.eu/index_en
DESCRIPTION Joanna Bryson challenges many popular assumptions about AI and AGI. She argues that current generative AI systems are not autonomous intelligences but powerful tools that compress and reproduce human cultural knowledge at scale. While these systems can appear intelligent, they remain dependent on human-created data, corporate incentives, and carefully engineered guardrails. Bryson cautions against anthropomorphizing AI and emphasizes that many concerns about AI stem from misunderstandings about how these systems actually work.
The discussion ultimately centers on responsibility and governance. Bryson argues that transparency, accountability, and democratic oversight matter far more than debates about machine consciousness. Whether discussing AI companions, recommendation algorithms, open-source models, regulation, or AI-assisted software development, her conclusion remains consistent: technology is not destiny. Engineers, companies, policymakers, and citizens all have agency in shaping how AI is deployed, regulated, and integrated into society. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 00:20 Is AGI just copy-paste? 07:05 Mental health, chatbots & Twitter 21:48 AI, Law & Transparency 31:00 Big data in different industries 34:34 Regulating tech giants 40:23 Teaching critical thinking to the next generations 44:47 Outro
Read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2025/sessions/3783
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Chris Miller • Chip War • https://amzn.to/4aBCUKT Henry Farrell & Abraham Newman • Underground Empire • https://amzn.to/3YdXdGX Phil Winder • Reinforcement Learning • https://amzn.to/3t1S1VZ Alex Castrounis • AI for People and Business • https://amzn.to/3NYKKTo
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #AI #CorporateResponsibility #DemocraticLegitimacy #DesignRequirements #DigitalSuperintelligence #Transparency #Polarization #Inequality #Diversity #GDPR #SystemsEngineering #TodayInTech #JoannaBryson #GOTO
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter

item.title: British Soldiers Captured This From the Taliban
item.author: Imperial War Museums
item.content:encoded:
This video examines a Taliban flag. IT was cptured by British forces in Nad-e-Ali in 2010.
Credit: © Joseph Coyne (MGH 6922-2) © Mark Kemp (MGH 6942-28) © Christopher Cook (MGH 6950-9) © Joseph Coyne (DC 18627)

item.title: Survival Strategies when confronting a Mongol invasion
item.author: Royal Armouries
item.content:encoded:
Survival Strategies when confronting a Mongol invasion.
Speaker: Dr Nicholas Morton, Associate Professor, Nottingham Trent University.
When a Mongol army crosses the frontier, what is your next move? Run? Fight? Submit? Negotiate? This lecture explores the various countermeasures deployed by civilizations across the Middle East when facing overthrow.

item.title: Pregnancy for Black Women in America | Full Documentary
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
Follow the story of three Black women navigating pregnancy within a system not built to protect them. The film "Listen to Me" challenges us to shift the narrative from crisis response to reimagine maternal health as a continuum of care, justice, healing and radical listening.
Every year, Black women in the U.S. are disproportionately affected by maternal health complications and mortality. "Listen to Me" offers an unfiltered, human lens into this crisis—creating space for deeper understanding and critical dialogue.
Listen to Me | Full Documentary
Find more from this series: https://www.pbs.org/show/afropop/
This program is made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Subscribe to the PBS channel for more clips: https://www.youtube.com/@PBS
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app: https://to.pbs.org/2QbtzhR

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PBS/ X: https://twitter.com/PBS/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/PBS/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@pbs Threads: https://www.threads.net/@pbs
#pregnancy #motherhood #parenting
AfroPoP: The Ultimate Cultural Exchange from Now celebrating its 18th Season, AfroPoP: The Ultimate Cultural Exchange remains the only U.S.-based public media show focused on independent documentaries and narrative films about contemporary life, art and culture found across the African diaspora. Black Public Media (@BlackPublicMedia) supports the development of visionary content creators and distributes stories about the global Black experience to inspire a more equitable and inclusive future. Find more from this series: https://www.pbs.org/show/afropop/

item.title: The Animal that ‘Invented’ Sex on Land | Evolution | BBC Earth Science
item.author: BBC Earth Science
item.content:encoded:
Chris Packham explores the evolution of copulation, with a slight detour on weird and wonderful animal genitalia…
Find out where to watch Evolution where you are 👉 https://www.bbcearth.com/shows/evolution

item.title: We Finally Know Where Saturn’s Rings Came From
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
How did Saturn get its rings? Thanks to Displate for sponsoring this video. Use code ASTRUM at checkout for exclusive discounts on your order at https://displate.com/l/astrum. Get 22% off 1 Displate, 27% off 2 Displates, or 33% off 3 Displates. Not valid on Limited Edition Displates.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Think Saturn was born with rings? Think again. Cassini data proved the iconic ring system formed only recently in Saturn’s life. And in this video, we’ll explore the brand-new 2026 research that explains exactly how it happened.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 Saturn's Rings 3:30 How Many Rings? 4:39 Shepherd Moons 9:00 The Roche Limit 10:10 RIP Chrysalis 11:41 Giant Collision 14:01 New Titan and Hyperion 15:55 Why Saturn is Tilted 17:15 How the Rings Formed 18:08 Dragonfly: New Mission to Saturn
▀▀▀▀▀▀
To stay on top of space news, sign up to the Astrum newsletter: https://astrumspace.kit.com
A huge thanks to our Patreons who help make these videos possible. Sign-up here to support the channel: https://bit.ly/4aiJZNF

▀▀▀▀▀▀

Astrum Earth: https://youtube.com/@astrumearth Astrum Extra: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumextra
Astrum Spanish: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumespanol Astrum Portuguese: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UChn_-OwvV63mr1yeUGvH-BQ
▀▀▀▀▀▀
References: “Saturn Facts”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/satfacts “Saturn: History of Discoveries”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/sathist “A Brief Astronomical History of Saturn’s Amazing Rings”, via usc.edu https://astrumspace.info/satrings “What Caused Saturn’s Rings and Tilt?”, via smithsonianmag.com https://astrumspace.info/satmoon “Loss of a Satellite Could Explain Saturn’s Obliquity and Young Rings”, via science.org https://astrumspace.info/satobliq “Origin of Hyperion and Saturn’s Rings”, via iop.org https://astrumspace.info/hypersaturn “The Past and Future Obliquity of Saturn as Titan Migrates”, via aanda.org https://astrumspace.info/titanmig “How Saturn Got Its Rings”, via mos.org https://astrumspace.info/satrings2 “Titanic Shake-Up Could Explain Saturn’s Young Rings and Strange Moons”, via eos.org https://astrumspace.info/satsystem “Dragonfly Mission”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/dragonfly
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Credits: Writer: Patricia Ward Video Editor: Suhith Audio Editor: James Horsley Researcher: Shourya Shrivastava Script Editor: Damaris McColgan Thumbnail Designers: Peter Sheppard, Juane Dames Publishing Lead: Georgina Brenner Production Coordinator: Rose Unwin Edit Producer: Poppy Pinnock Head of Astrum: Jess Jordan Creator of Astrum: Alex McColgan
With special thanks to: NASA/ESO/ESA
#Astrum #Space #Saturn

item.title: The Milwaukee Cannibal - "My Friend Dahmer" | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
The graphic novel "My Friend Dahmer” recounts the early years of a serial killer in the US. This deeply disturbing book by Derf Backderf, a school friend of Dahmer’s, delves into the depths of human madness.
Jeffrey Dahmer, one of the most feared serial killers in US history, was arrested in 1991. He had killed 17 men, most of them young, Black, and gay. When he learned of the crimes, Derf Backderf suddenly found himself confronted with his own past: at secondary school and sixth form, he had been on friendly terms with Dahmer, who was already considered an outsider at the time. To process his shock and make sense of it all, Backderf decided to write a graphic novel — a project spanning almost 20 years. "My Friend Dahmer” became one of the most significant works in the genre.
Drawing on previously unpublished archive footage and testimonies from Backderf’s friends, the documentary offers a deep insight into the long and sometimes painful process that went into creating this extraordinary book. It shows just how difficult it is to tell the story of a troubled teenager who appears to be like so many of his contemporaries, but who has a dark secret. How to maintain the necessary distance without succumbing to leniency or developing empathy for the future perpetrator? And how to confront one’s own past without harboring feelings of guilt? A difficult journey, that ultimately made Backderf one of the most famous contemporary comic artists in the US.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #serialkiller #jeffreydahmer ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. item.title: How H.H. Holmes Became America’s First Serial Killer
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Long before he was hanged in 1896 for murdering his business partner, Herman Webster Mudgett, better known as Dr. Henry Howard Holmes, had built a reputation for fraud, deception, and violence. He also built an infamous "Murder Castle" in Chicago, a labyrinthine building allegedly designed to lure and kill travelers attending the 1893 World's Fair. Historians have confirmed at least nine victims, but many believe the true death toll may have been far, far higher.
On this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift we separate the historical record from the sensational rumors surrounding H. H. Holmes, exploring the crimes, scams, and enduring mysteries that turned him into one of history's most notorious figures.
Do you believe the legends about H.H. Holmes and his"Murder Castle"? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#

item.title: Does sports betting need guardrails?
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 World Cup became the biggest betting event in history, but addiction and public health experts are sounding the alarm bells on the consequences of this rampant rise in sports gambling.
Since the Supreme Court removed the federal ban on sports betting in 2018, and with the rise of prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket, addiction experts have seen a measurable increase in young people seeking gambling addiction treatment. A 2026 Federal Reserve report found that states that legalized sports betting are seeing lower credit scores and increased auto delinquencies, especially among those under 40.
Is it time to add guardrails to the US sports betting industry as advertising, technology, and social media converge into a 24/7 cycle of gambling exposure?
If you or a loved one are experiencing distress from gambling, you can call 1-800-MY-RESET, the national gambling helpline that gets you connected to a counselor or therapist in your area.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: Key Maduro ally deported to U.S.
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded:
Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro's case could become one of the biggest narco-state prosecutions in U.S. history. And now, one of his closet allies is also in U.S. custody: Alex Saab.
Saab was charged in federal court in Miami with bribing top officials to profit from lucrative government contracts. FRONTLINE filmmaker Juan Ravell, who has followed Saab's movements for years, explains how his arrest could be used in the wider case against Maduro. To learn more, watch our 2025 film in partnership with @armando.info, "A Dangerous Assignment," which is now streaming on YouTube. Link in our bio.

item.title: Andrew Detained by IDF
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: When Cable TV Came to a Buddhist Kingdom (full documentary) | FRONTLINE/World
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded:
An exploration of the impact of television on a remote Buddhist kingdom in the Himalayas. (Aired 2002)
This journalism is made possible by viewers like you. Donate to FRONTLINE now: https://bit.ly/47DFzCb
And support your local PBS station here: https://www.pbs.org/donate
In 1999, Bhutan, a Buddhist kingdom that had remained isolated in the Himalayan mountains for centuries, became the last country in the world to legalize TV.
In the years that followed, Bhutan opened itself to what some critics called “an electronic invasion” — cable television. In this FRONTLINE/World short documentary that aired in 2002, reporters Alexis Bloom and Tshewang Dendup explored the differing reactions to the advent of cable TV in the Buddhist kingdom, including the perspective of a co-owner of a cable service company, who said, “We always have to move forward.”
“Bhutan: The Last Place” was produced by Alexis Bloom, who was also the videographer. The co-producer and videographer was Tshewang Dendup. The founder and executive producer of FRONTLINE was David Fanning.
We are making episodes from FRONTLINE/World’s archive newly available here on YouTube and on pbs.org as part of an effort to make our past reporting widely accessible.
Originally developed by FRONTLINE producers in conjunction with public television stations KQED San Francisco and WGBH Boston, FRONTLINE/World launched in 2002 as a national public TV series that turned its lens on the global community, covering countries and cultures rarely seen on American television. The series completed its final broadcast season in 2010.
Explore more about FRONTLINE/World and this film on the legacy website for the series: https://www.pbs.org/frontlineworld/
#Documentary #Bhutan #Television #Cable
Subscribe on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/user/PBSfrontline Sign up for our newsletter: https://frontline.org/newsletter Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/frontlinepbs​ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/frontline Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/frontlinepbs.bsky.social
FRONTLINE/World was a production of GBH in Boston. The executive producer was David Fanning.
FRONTLINE is produced at GBH in Boston and airs nationwide on PBS. The editor-in-chief and executive producer of FRONTLINE is Raney Aronson-Rath.
Funding for FRONTLINE is provided through the support of PBS viewers and by the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, with major support from Ford Foundation, and The Fialkow Family Foundation. Additional support for FRONTLINE is provided by the Abrams Foundation, Park Foundation, John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation, Heising-Simons Foundation, and the FRONTLINE Trust, with major support from Jon and Jo Ann Hagler on behalf of the Jon L. Hagler Foundation, and Corey David Sauer, and additional support from Koo and Patricia Yuen.
CHAPTERS:
00:00 - The Introduction of TV in Bhutan 03:28 - How Cable TV Impacted One Bhutanese Family 06:04 - TV’s Influence on Bhutanese Children 09:26 - Credits

item.title: The Final 100 Days Of WW2: How The Third Reich And Imperial Japan Collapsed
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
This documentary reveals the harrowing final chapter of WW2, exploring the dramatic collapse of Nazi Germany and Imperial Japan. From the desperate race to Berlin and Hitler’s final hours in the bunker to the bloody battle of Okinawa and the dawn of the atomic age, discover the true human cost of total war. Witness how the end of global conflict shattered empires and birthed the Cold War, told through rare archival footage and powerful eyewitness testimony.
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more. Get an exclusive release every week by signing up here: https://bit.ly/4pyExyn
This channel is part of the History Hit Network. Any queries, please contact owned-enquiries@littledotstudios.com

item.title: How the culture war came for condoms, PrEP, and HIV testing
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
In the two decades since the founding of The President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief, or PEPFAR, the program has saved an astounding 25 million people’s lives through lifesaving HIV therapies and prevented millions of babies from being born with HIV in the first place. But now under the Trump administration, it’s in danger.
Find out more: https://www.vox.com/future-perfect/496212/pepfar-hiv-trump-aid-amfar
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.title: Demystifying Spooky Action At A Distance, with Sean Hodgman
item.author: StarTalk
item.content:encoded:
Boldly Go One Step Closer To Where It All Began. Strange New Worlds S4 Streaming July 23 on Paramount+. https://www.paramountplus.com/shows/star-trek-strange-new-worlds/
T-Mobile 5G Home Internet. https://T-Mobile.com/HomeInternet
How do you get entangled particles? Neil deGrasse Tyson and comic co-host Chuck Nice unpack the experimental side of entanglement, superposition, and the quantum underpinnings of our universe with experimental physicist, Sean Hodgman.
How do you entangle particles? Can you entangle larger things, like atoms? Sean describes his job cooling helium atoms to a millionth of a degree above absolute zero to form a Bose-Einstein condensate. Learn about momentum entanglement and why entangling atoms rather than photons matters. Could entangling atoms open the window to quantum gravity?
What is superposition, really? Sean walks Chuck through quantum superpositions, Einstein’s criticisms of “spooky action at a distance,”and Bell’s Theorem. How do you actually entangle a particle, and how do you know it worked? What are the different subtypes of entanglement beyond momentum? Has studying entanglement changed how physicists understand reality? Could you ever use entanglement for faster-than-light communication? Why does the no-communication theorem say no, while quantum encryption says there's still something useful here?
The episode closes on the biggest questions: is there an upper size limit to entanglement? Could spacetime itself emerge from a network of entangled particles? Would we notice if entanglement were switched off and could quantum effects be quietly underpinning DNA stability, bird navigation, and photosynthesis?
Thanks to our Patrons Dominic B., Chirag Trasikar, Chase Dishman, Benjamin Rogers, Anthony Smith, Matt J, Bill Fisher, Gregory Shaw, Jesse Phelps, Shakil Chagani, Valentine, Sayrah rana, Linda Corbin, Kerry Gallagher, Emma Korein, Kate, Sara Drollinger, brad hodge, William Colt, Jill Campbell, Sai Dev Mahajan, Damien Hewlett, Ryan Christensen, Matthew Barrett, Pau Ferrer, Navindra Persaud, Felipe Marcondes, Deatric Wilson, Ashish Paka, Larry, BRUNO, Adán Rodríguez, Eneida Paulo, Todd P., Francesco Chionchio, Crouton, Anton Bursch, odamai, Brayden Malley, John Hamm, Tim Timrawi, Kuang kai Peter Tou, Adam Fry, Raylen, Pele_Glendale, Balázs Pető, Sergiu Calin, Shane King, Torstein Sundnes, Deonne, Mark Bailey, Rodrigo Gómez, Bri, Richard Riley, James Gray, adam, Kyle, Tam Lee, Liz Schwabedissen, Jim Dulian, and Jesse for supporting us this week.
Timestamps: 00:00 - Introduction: Sean Hodgeman 01:39 - Creating A Bose-Einstein Condensate 8:10 - How to Entangle Atoms 10:52 - How Long Do They Stay Entangled? 14:39 - Understanding Superpositions 16:40 - Bell Inequality Theorem & Interference Patterns 19:55 - How Do You Know It’s Entangled 23:51 - Subtypes of Entanglement 27:18 - Entanglement & Reality 30:42 - Is There a Size Limit to Entanglement? 33:48 - Subspace Communication 37:24 - Is The Universe Woven Out of Entangled Particles? 42:27 - Quantum Fluctuations Effect on Entanglement 45:05 - If Particle Entanglement Stopped 47:50 - Why Does Distance Matter For Spooky Action at a Distance? 49:54 - Not Messing it Up 52:49 - Math vs. Experiments vs. Philosophy 56:40 - How Does a Particle with Mass Function as a Wave Cloud 58:33 - Leading the entanglement
Check out our second channel, @StarTalkPlus
Get the latest StarTalk book, 'To Infinity and Beyond: A Journey of Cosmic Discovery' on Amazon: https://amzn.to/3PL0NFn

item.title: Winning Dogs Still Lose
item.author: Horses
item.content:encoded: Find more at: ⁠https://horses.land

item.title: The Berlin rental rip-off: The housing crisis boom on tiny rooms | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Just under 6 square meters for 600 euros a month. People moving to Berlin often find only overpriced, furnished short-term rooms available. It's all due to the city's housing shortage: Germany has rent cap laws, but they don't apply to temporary, furnished accommodation.
Some landlords even take it a step further. With drywall partitions, they're dividing existing apartments into multiple tiny rooms. What was once a four-bedroom apartment now has eight people crammed into it. It's often younger people moving to Berlin who face the worst of it. Now, the boroughs of Neukölln and Pankow are tightening regulations on room rentals and expanding neighborhood protection measures to crack down on the business model. Along with tenant activists, newcomers are pushing back against high rents and sketchy rental practices. The fight is on for affordable housing and the chance to build a future in Berlin.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #berlin #housingcrisis ______

item.content:encoded:

item.title: The Most Controversial Thought Experiment In Physics
item.author: Veritasium
item.content:encoded:
Put a cat in a box with a radioactive atom. Add a radiation detector that triggers the release of poisonous cyanide gas.
Schrödinger's thought experiment was supposed to be a criticism of quantum mechanics and is now one of it's best explainers.
Here's why and what happens to the cat...

item.title: We Made a TV Show!
item.author: PBS Eons
item.content:encoded:
Hey Eons fans, after 8 seasons of exploring deep time we have some very important news to share...
we've secretly been working on more stuff! We made a TV Show! Eons: Life and Death on Pangea. The series premieres with 2 episodes dropping on Wednesday, July 29 on the PBS app, website, and other platforms and the very first episode will also live here on Youtube.
Get the PBS App to watch Eons: Life and Death on Pangea for free starting July 29th! https://to.pbs.org/EonsTeaser
If you're outside of the US and want to watch the show, you can check it out on PBS.org. https://pbs.org/eons
We'll also be hosting a watch party of episode 2 here on YouTube on August 4th at 4pmET / 1pm PT.
We're so excited to share this series with you.
***** PBS Member Stations rely on viewers like you. To support your local station, go to http://to.pbs.org/DonateEons *****
Eons is a production of Complexly for PBS Digital Studios.
Super special thanks to the following Patreon patrons for helping make Eons possible: Scezumin, Mike Cumings, Jr., NassauLinda, Chuck Smith, DexcilaDou, Martin G. Diller, Johnathan Williams, Allison Wood, Katrix , Jason Terpstra, Jennifer Wiggins-Lyndall, Dalton Williams, Chelsea S, Matthew Fredericksen, AThirstyPhilosopher ., Michael Maher, Gina Mancuso, Roger Harms, Shruti S, Quinn Harden, Reed Spilmann, Brandon Thomas, Emily Beazley, Rie Ohta, oranjeez, UwU, Elizabeth LaBelle, Leah H., David Fanska, Andrew Woods, Kevin Knupp, Barbara Pettersen, Ken Davidian, Stephen Akuffo, Toni Miles, Steve Segreto, Kyle & Katherine Callahan, Laurel Stevens, Tanner Hedrick, Kristina D Knight, Samantha, Krystle Young, Perry Joyce, Scott Harrison, Alan Bridgeman, Breanna Bosso, Matt Curls, Jennifer Killen, Sarah & Nathan Catchings, team dorsey, Bernardo Garza, Trevin Beattie, Pietro Gagliardi, John Lee, Eric Koslow, Indija-ka Siriwardena, Jason Rostoker, Siobhán, Ken Penttinen, Nathan Taylor, Barrett, Les Aker, Rizwan Kassim, Constance Urist, Alex Hackman, Triad Terrace, Katie Dean, Jason Buster, Emily T, Stephen McCandless, Thomas, Joseph Ruf, Wai Jack Sin, Ian Dundore, Erminio Di Lodovico, Evol Hong, Tandy Ratliff, Caleb Weeks, Luke Sluder
If you'd like to support the channel, head over to http://patreon.com/eons and pledge for some cool rewards!

item.title: The mystery of the giant kelp forest - Luka Seamus Wright and Salomé Buglass
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Discover the ocean’s hidden kelp forests, and explore how these light-dependent organisms evolved to survive at dark depths.
--
Like any other plant, kelps require light to grow. Yet kelps found nearly 90 meters below the ocean's surface, where sometimes only 0.1% of sunlight can reach, grow as large as, and often even larger than, their shallow counterparts. So how have these large, light-dependent organisms adapted to thrive at such depths? Luka Seamus Wright and Salomé Buglass explore the ocean's hidden forests.
Lesson by Luka Seamus Wright and Salomé Buglass, directed by Upamanyu Bhattacharyya, Otter Studios.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------
Connect With Us ---------------------------------------------- Sign up for our newsletter: http://bit.ly/TEDEdNewsletter Follow us on Facebook: http://bit.ly/TEDEdFacebook Find us on Twitter: http://bit.ly/TEDEdTwitter Peep us on Instagram: http://bit.ly/TEDEdInstagram ----------------------------------------------
Keep Learning ---------------------------------------------- View full lesson: https://ed.ted.com/lessons/the-gigantic-forests-you-wont-find-on-land-luka-seamus-wright-and-salome-buglass Dig deeper with additional resources: https://ed.ted.com/lessons/the-gigantic-forests-you-wont-find-on-land-luka-seamus-wright-and-salome-buglass/digdeeper ----------------------------------------------
Thank you so much to our patrons for your support! Without you this video would not be possible! Liz Candee, Ugur Doga Sezgin, Karmi Nguyen, John C. Vesey, Yelena Baykova, Nick Johnson, Carlos H. Costa, Jennifer Kurkoski, Ryan B Harvey, Akinola Emmanuel, Jose Arcadio Valdes Franco, Sebastiaan Vleugels, Karl Laius, JY Kang, Abhishek Goel, Heidi Stolt, Nicole Sund, Karlee Finch, Mario Mejia, Denise A Pitts, Doug Henry, Keven Webb, Mihai Sandu, Deepak Iyer, Javid Gozalov, Kyanta Yap, Rebecca Reineke, William Biersdorf, Patricia Alves Panagides, Yvette Mocete, Cyrus Garay, Samuel Barbas, LadyGeek, Marin Kovachev, Penelope Misquitta, Hans Peng, Gaurav Mathur, Erik Biemans, Tony, Michelle, Katie and Josh Pedretti, Hoai Nam Tran, Kack-Kyun Kim, Michael Braun-Boghos, zjweele13, Anna-Pitschna Kunz, Edla Paniguel, Thomas Mungavan, Jaron Blackburn, Venkat Venkatakrishnan, ReuniteKorea, and Aaron Henson.

item.title: What does gut health really mean?
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Worried about the spread of the diarrhea-causing parasite cyclosporiasis? A healthier gut might better prepare your body to deal with this illness. With all the noise of wellness gurus and coaches, we decided to cut through the noise and go directly to researchers who have been studying gut health and the human microbiome for decades.
Gut health is everywhere right now. The hashtag #guttok has over 6 billion views on TikTok, the global digestive health product market is worth over $60 billion, and viral trends that promise to “hack your gut” through miracle products, cleanses, and restrictive diets are all the rage. But what does gut health really mean and what can science tell us about how to make sure our gut is in good shape? #guthealth #gutmicrobiome
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: The truth about gut health
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Worried about the spread of the diarrhea-causing illness cyclosporiasis? A healthier gut might better prepare your body to deal with it. With all the noise of wellness experts and coaches, we decided to go directly to doctors and researchers who have been studying gut health and the human microbiome for decades.
Gut health is everywhere right now. The hashtag #GutTok has over 6 billion views on TikTok, the global digestive health product market is worth over $60 billion, and viral trends that promise to “hack your gut” through miracle products, cleanses, and restrictive diets are all the rage. But what does gut health really mean, and what can science tell us about how to make sure our gut is in good shape?
With so many voices chiming in on gut health, pushing everything from probiotic foods and supplements to wellness shots and coffee enemas, it can be hard to tell what information is based on real research and what might just be a marketing gimmick or social media clickbait. Vox producer Benjamin Stephen spoke to doctors and visited Cedars-Sinai in Los Angeles to bust some gut health myths for science-backed advice.
Read more about gut health and gut microbiome research:
Vox editor Katherine Courage’s book, Cultured: How Ancient Foods Can Feed Our Microbiome: https://katherinecourage.com/book/
Cedars-Sinai’s Human Microbiome Research Institute: https://www.cedars-sinai.edu/health-sciences-university/research/departments-institutes/medicine/human-microbiome.html
The MAST program at Cedars-Sinai: https://csmast.com/
Trisha Pasricha’s book, You’ve Been Pooping All Wrong: https://www.trishapasricha.com/youvebeenpoopingallwrong
Vox’s explainer video on probiotics in yogurt: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sYj09lx15ik
Pew Research’s findings on why people look to influencers for health and wellness information: https://www.pewresearch.org/data-labs/2026/05/07/why-do-americans-get-information-from-health-and-wellness-influencers-and-how-do-they-find-them/
The Myths and Scientific Realities About Gut Microbiota: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12525918/
This video is presented by Colgate. Colgate doesn’t have a say in our editorial decisions, but they make videos like this one possible.
If you enjoy our reporting and want to hear more from Vox journalists, sign up for our Patreon at patreon.com/vox. Each month, our members get access to exclusive videos, livestreams, and chats with our newsroom.
Subscribe to our channel! http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on Facebook: http://goo.gl/U2g06o Or Twitter: http://goo.gl/XFrZ5H

item.title: WebAssembly on Kubernetes • Nicolas Frankel • YOW! 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at YOW! Australia 2025. #GOTOcon #YOW https://yowcon.com
Nicolas Fränkel - Developer & Architect @NicolasFrankel
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/frankel.ch https://mastodon.top/@frankel https://twitter.com/nicolas_frankel https://github.com/nfrankel https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicolasfrankel https://blog.frankel.ch
Links https://kubernetes.o https://webassembly.org https://youtu.be/yabE04SD5bQ https://wasmer.io/posts/python-on-the-edge-powered-by-webassembly https://www.fermyon.com/blog/how-to-think-about-wasm https://wasmedge.org/docs/develop/deploy/intro
ABSTRACT WebAssembly started as a technology tailored to web browsers and is becoming popular as a server-side technology as well. The next step is for Wasm to become a powerful tool for cloud-native applications. When combined with Kubernetes, WebAssembly can revolutionize application deployment, security, and resource efficiency in ways traditional containers cannot.
This talk explores why and how to leverage WebAssembly within Kubernetes environments to create scalable, high-performance, and secure applications. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 02:14 WebAssembly in 2018 05:00 WebAssembly in 2020 07:30 WebAssembly now 10:05 The many moving facets of WASM on k8s 11:00 The demo points today 14:20 Demo 24:07 Just enough k8s intrinsics 25:44 WASMedge 26:11 Demo 33:32 The good, the bad & the ugly 35:44 Summary 36:21 Outro
Download slides and read the full abstract here: https://yowcon.com/brisbane-2025/sessions/3619
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Kevin Hoffman • Programming WebAssembly with Rust • https://amzn.to/48msEBz Valerio De Sanctis • Building Web APIs with ASP.NET Core • https://amzn.to/42MWuOq Brian Sletten • WebAssembly: The Definitive Guide • https://amzn.to/3OQdHRf Sendil Kumar Nellaiyapen • Practical WebAssembly • https://amzn.to/4bK3j7s
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #WebAssembly #WASM #Kubernetes #k8s #CloudNative #Programming #SoftwareEngineering #TodayInTech #NicolasFrankel #YOWcon
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: The questions fossils leave behind
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
The study of dinosaurs — also known as paleontology — is built on questions. How did they move? What did they look like? Paleontologist Riley Black talks to Unexplainable host Byrd Pinkerton about why dinosaurs combine science and imagination.
Watch on Netflix, or listen whenever you get your podcasts.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: Inside Gandhi's Private Ashram: Rare Access To The Mahatma's Room
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
Delve into the complex truth behind Mahatma Gandhi's rise to power and his fight against the British Raj. From his spiritual home at Sabarmati Ashram to the horrifying bloodshed of the 1919 Amritsar Massacre, discover how Gandhi’s philosophy of Satyagraha challenged imperial rule. We explore the paradox of his nonviolent campaigns, his bitter political rivalries, and the historic 1930 Salt March that shook the foundations of an empire. This is the untold story of the man behind the myth.
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more. Get an exclusive release every week by signing up here: https://bit.ly/4pyExyn
This channel is part of the History Hit Network. Any queries, please contact owned-enquiries@littledotstudios.com

item.title: Yes, you can avoid hot parasite summer
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Let’s be real, we’re all slightly living in fear of contracting the Cyclospora parasite. So what can you do to protect yourself? Start by thoroughly washing your vegetables and cooking them to kill any potentially harmful bacteria.
Read more: https://www.vox.com/health/495831/cyclospora-parasite-diarrhea-outbreak-food-avoid-taco-bell
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: How do we know what dinosaurs were really like?
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
None of us were alive when the dinosaurs roamed, so how do we know so much about what they were like? Paleontologist Riley Black is our guest on this week’s episode of Unexplainable, and he explains how we came to find out about these prehistoric creatures.
Watch on Netflix, or listen whenever you get your podcasts.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: Should You Be Worried About Flying Frontier?
item.author: StarTalk
item.content:encoded:
Order Take Me to Your Leader here 👇 https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/Take-Me-to-Your-Leader/Neil-deGrasse-Tyson/9781668249970

About StarTalk: Science meets pop culture on StarTalk! Astrophysicist & Hayden Planetarium director Neil deGrasse Tyson, his comic co-hosts, guest celebrities & scientists discuss astronomy, physics, and everything else about life in the universe. Keep Looking Up!
#StarTalk #NeildeGrasseTyson

item.title: What Did 'Inglourious Basterds' Get Right About WW2?
item.author: History Hit
item.content:encoded:
We love it, but can any historical truths be found in Tarantino's epic Inglourious Basterds??
War historian Jennifer Howe takes us through the real stories of the SS, Nazi occupation and The Richie boys, portrayed in this movie by Christoph Waltz, Brad Pitt, Diane Kruger, Eli Roth, and Mélanie Laurent.
0:00 Introduction 0:36 Overview of Inglourious Basterds 1:27 Historical context of the 1941 occupied France 2:54 Analysing Christoph Waltz's Hans Landa 4:54 Bureaucracy and systems of the Holocaust 6:15 Nazi propaganda and antisemitic rhetoric 7:59 Were German officers polite? 9:58 Did the SS knock on doors? 11:54 Real behind-the-lines missions and the Richie Boys 14:34 The myth of scalping 16:12 The mental state and decline of Adolf Hitler 19:06 We meet Shosanna hiding as Emmanuelle 22:51 Joseph Goebbels and Nazi propaganda films 27:33 Hans Landa interrogation scene 31:31 America's protection of Nazis 33:00 Kolberh (1945) Propaganda film 34:03 The Nazis burn in the theatre, ending the war?
#WW2 #InglouriousBasterds #MovieReview
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world-renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more.
Get an exclusive release every week by signing up here: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCZwU2G-KVl-P-O-B35chZOQ/join
